theory.hypothesis: Reina's silent-letter spelling depends on explicit orthographic memory for kn-, wr-, -mb, and gn- patterns, while her high-frequency word reading depends on whole-word visual recognition. The two groups require different measurement instruments: spelling construction for silent letters (because the teacher heading and pattern focus signal production goals) and visible-read pronunciation for high-frequency words (because the teacher heading signals recognition/fluency goals, not spelling production).
evidenceSummary: Source document separates two labeled groups with different teacher-assigned purposes
Silent Letters group contains pattern-focused words (kn-, wr-, -mb, gn-) requiring orthographic production memory
High-Frequency Words group contains Benchmark Advance sight vocabulary for reading fluency, not spelling drill
No prior session evidence exists for Reina; this is the first baseline session
masteryContext shows 2 days until test with urgent intensity, requiring fast baseline evidence and efficient coverage
theory.intervention: Measure silent-letter spelling using Word Radar partial visual recall (letter-by-letter construction with hidden word) to capture per-word accuracy and error patterns. Measure high-frequency word recognition using Pronunciation visible read mode to capture fluency and decoding, not spelling production. Offer route choice after both baselines: Route A extends silent-letter evidence with Spell Check audio-prompted spelling; Route B extends high-frequency evidence with Word Radar visible-read flow. Mystery provides preference evidence without contaminating mastery. Quest and Boss remain locked until baseline and quest evidence justify unlocking.
supportCriteria: Silent-letter baseline shows ≥7/10 correct first try, suggesting pattern is accessible and route practice can deepen encoding
High-frequency baseline shows ≥8/10 correct pronunciation, suggesting recognition is strong and visible-read flow can build automaticity
Route choice shows clear preference and completion, confirming agency model fits Reina's engagement style
Mystery completion with positive affect confirms reward timing and type are appropriate
reviseCriteria: Silent-letter baseline shows 3-6/10 correct or multiple retries, indicating pattern needs explicit teaching before more construction drills
High-frequency baseline shows 4-7/10 correct or hesitation, indicating recognition needs more visible-read repetition before fluency demands
Route choice shows skip or abandonment, suggesting route options need revision or stamina is lower than expected
Mystery shows neutral or skip, indicating reward type or timing mismatch
falsifyCriteria: Silent-letter baseline shows ≤2/10 correct or refusal, indicating silent-letter pattern is not accessible and construction drills are premature
High-frequency baseline shows ≤3/10 correct or decoding struggle, indicating sight vocabulary is not yet automatic and needs direct teaching
Route choice shows refusal or frustration, indicating agency model is wrong for Reina or baseline work was too hard
Mystery shows negative affect or early exit, indicating reward is aversive or mistimed

plannedMeasurements.supportCriteria: ≥7 words correct first try with low latency and no help requests confirms baseline competence
plannedMeasurements.reviseCriteria: 3-6 words correct or multiple retries/help requests indicates need for audio-prompted support and pattern teaching
plannedMeasurements.falsifyCriteria: ≤2 words correct or refusal/frustration indicates silent-letter pattern is not yet accessible and needs explicit teaching before more drills

plannedMeasurements.supportCriteria: ≥8 words pronounced correctly first try with fluency confirms recognition baseline
plannedMeasurements.reviseCriteria: 4-7 words correct or hesitation/skips indicates need for visible-read repetition and fluency support
plannedMeasurements.falsifyCriteria: ≤3 words correct or decoding struggle indicates high-frequency words are not yet automatic and need direct teaching

plannedMeasurements.supportCriteria: Child chooses and completes chosen route with engagement and low frustration
plannedMeasurements.reviseCriteria: Child skips or abandons chosen route, suggesting route mismatch or fatigue
plannedMeasurements.falsifyCriteria: Child refuses choice or shows frustration at gate, indicating agency model needs revision

plannedMeasurements.supportCriteria: Child engages with mystery content and completes with positive affect
plannedMeasurements.reviseCriteria: Child skips or shows neutral engagement, suggesting reward type mismatch
plannedMeasurements.falsifyCriteria: Child refuses or shows negative affect, indicating reward timing or type needs revision
